findings.message: D6 requires updating the roadmap ADR front matter with `superseded_by`, but the related external_guides ADR explicitly says that same roadmap ADR is grandfathered/post-merge and must not receive semantic front-matter changes. That makes the supersede mechanism contradictory; the plan should use a permitted back-reference/new-ADR encoding instead of requiring a forbidden roadmap front-matter update.

The Makefile migration instructions conflict for `conventions-verify-index`: one bullet says `conventions-*` wrapper tasks should be retained with their implementation swapped to Rust, while the next says the `conventions-verify-index` wrapper should be deleted. This leaves the implementation boundary ambiguous for an existing public task name.

The ADR keeps non-git `cargo make` wrappers (`conventions-*`, `architecture-rules-*`, `workspace-tree*`) and only rewires them to Rust commands, but the already indexed cargo-make teardown ADR says native subcommands that can be called directly should not be wrapped by `cargo make`; non-git pass-through wrappers should move to direct `bin/sotp <native>` usage and be deleted. These plan artifacts now give contradictory implementation direction for the same wrapper layer, so implementers could preserve tasks that the newer workflow ADR requires removing.

S1 claims the infrastructure work is "purely additive" and that no existing code is removed yet, but T002 in the same plan deletes `libs/infrastructure/src/verify/convention_docs.rs` and removes its module entry. The section-level sequencing claim is false and should be rewritten to acknowledge the relocation/deletion step.

`update_convention_index` and `verify_convention_index` are emitted with the same Mermaid node ID (`F82`), so the second node overwrites the first and the contract map no longer faithfully represents the catalogue.

`doc_patterns.rs` is called out in CN-04 as part of the same scripts-deletion / CI-verification sync change, but this plan defers any `doc_patterns.rs` update to T006 while T005 is defined as the only commit that must satisfy CN-04. That splits a required same-commit synchronization across two commits and makes the plan violate its own constraint as written.

`update_convention_index` and `verify_convention_index` are rendered with the same Mermaid node id (`F82_infrastructure_infrastructure_infrastructure__conventions__...`). That collapses two distinct functions into one node, and `verify_convention_index` no longer has its own edge to `ConventionDocsError`.

T005 removes the orchestra/settings allow-list entries for the `architecture-rules-*` and `conventions-*` wrapper task names, but those Makefile wrappers are only updated later in T006. Unless T005 also deletes or rewrites those tasks, the intermediate commit cannot stay CI-green and will fail the stated `verify-orchestra`/CI requirement.

`task-coverage.json` under-maps the test criteria: `AC-07` is assigned only to T001/T002, but `plan.md` assigns the required CLI integration roundtrip tests to T003/T004 as well. That makes the coverage matrix internally inconsistent and understates which tasks satisfy the test-related requirements (the same gap exists for `IN-07` at line 10).

`F82` is reused for both `update_convention_index` and `verify_convention_index`. In the Mermaid contract map, the second definition overwrites the first, so `verify_convention_index` does not have its own node/edge and the catalogue is no longer accurate.

T002 deletes `libs/infrastructure/src/verify/convention_docs.rs` and removes its verify module entry while explicitly deferring CLI wiring; T004 is later tasked with replacing the existing verify dispatch that calls `infrastructure::verify::convention_docs::verify`. That ordering leaves the tree broken after T002 and contradicts the S1 claim that T002 can be written/tested independently. Move the verify dispatch update or a compatibility shim into T002, or stop deleting the old module until T004.

`impl-plan.json` now treats the metadata-only aliases `T008`-`T014` as real implementation tasks and assigns all of them to S1. That contradicts the plan narrative and coverage, which define the executable chain as `T001`-`T007` with only `T001`/`T002` in S1; the rendered plan consequently shows `0/14` tasks and places CLI/removal/docs/ADR work under the infrastructure section. This makes task execution/status ambiguous rather than a harmless metadata alias. Keep the alias tasks out of the impl-plan task list/section task_ids, or otherwise render them only as references to `T001`-`T007`.
